prerequisites: A Formbricks account with access to an organization or project (Formbricks Cloud at app.formbricks.com or a self-hosted Formbricks instance).
Permission to open the Formbricks Settings and create an API key.
A generated Formbricks API key, which is the only credential required by this connector (config field: api_key).

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Formbricks authenticates every request with an API key sent in the x-api-key request header. This connector maps that key to its single required secret field, api_key. Keys are created in the Formbricks Settings under API Keys and can be granted Read, Write, or Manage permission levels; a Read-level key is sufficient for a source connector that only extracts data.

setupSteps.title: Sign in to Formbricks
setupSteps.body: Log in to your Formbricks account, either on Formbricks Cloud (app.formbricks.com) or your self-hosted instance, with a user that has access to the organization or project you want to sync.

setupSteps.title: Open Settings and go to API Keys
setupSteps.body: In the Formbricks app, open Settings and select the API Keys section, where you can view and manage existing keys and create new ones.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key
setupSteps.body: Add a new API key, give it a recognizable name, and assign its permission level. Read-only access is enough for this source connector, since it only extracts data. Save the key and copy its value.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied key into the connector's required api_key field. The connector sends it in the x-api-key header on every request to the Formbricks API.

sources.title: Formbricks Test API Key reference (x-api-key header)
Formbricks API Authentication (x-api-key header, Read/Write/Manage permissions, Settings > API Keys)
